content.content:
# Data Mesh Operations

This section covers the practical aspects of setting up, configuring, and operating your MeshWeaver data mesh.

## Overview

The Data Mesh operational guides provide hands-on documentation for:
- **Node Type Configuration** - Defining and customizing mesh node types
- **Query Syntax** - Filtering and retrieving data from the mesh
- **Unified Content References** - Working with UCR links and references
- **Interactive Markdown** - Creating dynamic, data-driven documents
- **Collaborative Editing** - Enabling real-time collaboration features

## Getting Started

To begin working with the data mesh, start with [Node Type Configuration](MeshWeaver/Documentation/DataMesh/NodeTypeConfiguration) to understand how to define your data structures, then explore [Query Syntax](MeshWeaver/Documentation/DataMesh/QuerySyntax) to learn how to filter and retrieve data.

## Guides

### Node Type Configuration
Learn how to define node types, configure properties, and customize behavior.

[Read Guide](MeshWeaver/Documentation/DataMesh/NodeTypeConfiguration)

---

### Query Syntax
Master the query syntax for filtering and retrieving mesh data.

[Read Guide](MeshWeaver/Documentation/DataMesh/QuerySyntax)

---

### Unified Content References
Understand how UCR links work and how to create references between content.

[Read Guide](MeshWeaver/Documentation/DataMesh/UnifiedContentReferences)

---

### Interactive Markdown
Create dynamic documents with embedded data and interactive components.

[Read Guide](MeshWeaver/Documentation/DataMesh/InteractiveMarkdown)

---

### Collaborative Editing
Enable real-time collaboration and concurrent editing features.

[Read Guide](MeshWeaver/Documentation/DataMesh/CollaborativeEditing)
